assertions.title: Email/password sign-up validates input and lands in the signed-in library
assertions.reasoning: I could not start the required chrome-devtools session in the assigned isolated context because the shared chrome-devtools profile was already locked by another running browser instance. That prevented opening the brainstorm route, creating the required unique Firebase account, exercising invalid and successful sign-up states, or capturing UI evidence.

steps.action: Open chrome-devtools at http://127.0.0.1:5173/#/brainstorm in isolated context brainstorm-auth-entry-email-library
steps.expected: The brainstorm entry modal loads so the email sign-up form can be tested
steps.observed: chrome-devtools failed before any page opened because /Users/dks0662779/.cache/chrome-devtools-mcp/chrome-profile was already in use

steps.action: Retry the same chrome-devtools open after a 15 second wait
steps.expected: The shared browser becomes available without disrupting other workers
steps.observed: The same profile-lock error was returned again

evidence.consoleFindings: blocked: no browser page or console session was available
requestResponseObservations: No in-browser auth request could be observed because no page session could be created

assertions.issues: Blocked by a shared chrome-devtools profile collision before any browser interaction

assertions.title: Returning signed-in users re-enter through the brainstorm library only
assertions.reasoning: This assertion needs a working browser session, persisted auth state, a revisit or reload of /#/brainstorm, and video/screenshot evidence showing the library-first modal state without auth-form or workspace flash. Because chrome-devtools could not open any page, none of that validation was possible.

assertions.title: Library delete removes only the selected session and prevents reopen
assertions.reasoning: This assertion requires creating multiple saved sessions, deleting one, and confirming the others remain and the deleted one cannot reopen. None of those browser steps could be exercised because chrome-devtools could not create a page.

assertions.reasoning: This cross-flow assertion requires observing the browser transition after successful sign-up or sign-in and confirming there is no direct workspace drop. With chrome-devtools unable to open any page, I could not test or record any auth-success transition.

frictions.description: chrome-devtools could not start because /Users/dks0662779/.cache/chrome-devtools-mcp/chrome-profile was already locked by another running browser instance
frictions.resolution: Retried browser startup after a 15 second wait and used curl to confirm the app itself was reachable. I did not kill or restart shared browser processes because mission guidance only allows non-disruptive fixes.

blockers.description: Required browser surface unavailable: chrome-devtools could not open any page because the shared browser profile was already in use
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried chrome-devtools startup twice, including after a 15 second wait, and confirmed frontend/backend reachability with curl. No disruptive browser reset was attempted.

summary: Attempted to validate 6 browser assertions, but all 6 were blocked because chrome-devtools could not start a page in the required isolated context due to a shared profile lock. The local frontend and backend were reachable via curl, but no browser UI, console, screenshots, or in-browser network evidence could be collected.
